textPlain: I see Ghostty does not support (and does not plan on adding support for) Sixels, instead preferring the Kitty image protocol.Now if the Kitty image protocol is so great and the Sixel stuff is so bad, ~~why is it only used in Kitty and Ghostty?~~*Edit: it's also supported in Konsole, WezTerm, ... but still I'm interested in why we have 2 competing protocols right now.
The current state of reddit WezTerm shills
So the state of 2025 then tests a VTE that is from 2023? 4 major releases behind? And through a GTK 3 app, not even a GTK 4 one which will use the GPU?
The terminal that comes with macOS version ends on the 29th place in the results.

I wonder how long until terminals support half of the XWindows protocol (as some weird combination of Markdown, HTML and escape codes, most probably). This is not a diss, I would actually be pretty happy with a pared-down GUI protocol in the terminal with extensive Unicode support.
Nothing even mentioned on WezTerm really?
More than two, e.g. there’s also the Inline Images Protocol supported by iTerm2 and WezTerm.Kovid documented his rationale at some length here: https://github.com/kovidgoyal/kitty/issues/33

We actually have 3 competing protocols: there is also the iTerm2 protocol, which is some kind of middle ground. It's plain PNG bytes (compared to the arcane format and 256 colo palette limit of sixel), but statelessly "dumped" into the terminal like sixels (not memory-shared nor dynamic like Kitty). The protocol is implemented in several other terminal emulators, like WezTerm, Rio, and Bobcat.Kitty is also implemented on WezTerm at least.

> Now if the Kitty image protocol is so great and the Sixel stuff is so bad, why is it only used in Kitty and Ghostty?Images as in "pictures" or is that something else? I'm using Alacritty, and I don't think I've once thought "I need to see this image inside the terminal" and I do deal with images and frames from videos a lot. Probably if I saw it being added to Alacritty I'd think it was adding unnecessary bloat, so I wouldn't be surprised not every terminal is rushing to implement it.Or I completely misunderstand what you're talking about.
Curious, what do you do with this?
I think it's because Kitty and Ghostty are the newest terminals, so they came up with new modern options and solutions.

Which one is that about specifically? Maybe the author could fix it.Compared the results (https://ucs-detect.readthedocs.io/results.html#general-tabul...) with what I use day-to-day (Alacritty) and seems the results were created with the same version I have locally installed, from Arch/CachyOS repos, namely 0.16.1 (42f49eeb).
Likewise I noticed that Konsole was version 23.08. I've just submitted a PR (https://github.com/jquast/ucs-detect/pull/14) to update it to 25.08.
Yeah… Apple hasn't done much with Terminal.app since they inherited it from NeXT back in the late '90s.FWIW, it did get Powerline support and 24-bit color in macOS 26.
and terminal that comes with Windows is on the 4th place.

2052: the whole of computing is VT100-compatible Javascript CLI applications running on a Javascript port of the Linux kernel, within a tab of Chromium.This is the actual end game of the worse is better philosophy.
It's in the results, even if not mentioned in the blogpost: https://ucs-detect.readthedocs.io/results.html#general-tabul...
